evals.prompt: I just built a refund API endpoint. How do I write proper tests for it? The rule is: can only refund within 30 days, order must be 'completed', and amount can't exceed the order total.
evals.expected_output: Agent produces 4+ pytest-asyncio tests: one per business rule failure (30-day window, wrong status, amount too high), plus one happy path test that asserts order status changes to 'refunded'. Tests use clean_db fixture, httpx AsyncClient, assert status codes and response body.

evals.prompt: Write unit tests for my <ProductCard /> React component. It shows a product image, name, price, and an 'Add to Cart' button. When clicked, it calls onAddToCart(productId).
evals.expected_output: Agent uses Vitest + React Testing Library. Tests: renders name, image (via alt text), price; clicking the button calls onAddToCart with the correct productId; button is accessible (getByRole). Uses vi.fn() for the mock callback.

evals.prompt: Set up end-to-end tests for our signup flow. New user signs up -> receives verification email -> clicks link -> lands on dashboard.
evals.expected_output: Agent creates playwright.config.ts, writes e2e/signup.spec.ts. Uses page.getByLabel() and page.getByRole() for form interaction. For email verification, mocks the email sending (or uses Mailpit in CI) and intercepts the token. Asserts final URL is /dashboard.
